pattern: Repeatedly shrinking only the current maximum is a simulation problem tailor-made for a max-heap, since the pile that needs updating each round is always the top of the heap.
hints: You only ever need to touch the single largest pile each round, so keep the piles in a structure that gives you the maximum quickly.
A max-heap (or a min-heap of negated values) lets you pop the largest pile, shrink it to the floor of its square root, and push it back in each of the k rounds.
After k rounds, the answer is simply the sum of whatever remains across all piles.

edge_inputs.description: single pile shrinking repeatedly toward one
multiple piles tied for the current maximum
maximum possible value shrinks toward one within very few rounds even with many rounds allotted
a pile already at one never changes once reached
k equals one, only the single largest pile changes
perfect squares shrink cleanly while others leave a remainder

stress.description: random piles of random size shrunk for a random number of rounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    gifts = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    return [gifts, k]
